JFrog already partnered at GTC 2026.
- fact · [Skills registry/catalog competitive landscape (2026-07-21)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-competitive-landscape-2026.md) — Comprehensive competitive landscape -- cloud platforms (AWS AgentCore, Azure Agent 365, Google Gemini Enterprise, Databricks Unity AI), frameworks (Anthropic Marketplace ~600 skills, skills.sh 669K+, HF 40K+ with ARD spec, LangChain/LangSmith), security specialists (JFrog+NVIDIA, Cisco DefenseClaw, Credo AI, Zenity, TrueFoundry); SKILL.md is the standard (16+ tools), EU AI Act high-risk Aug 2 2026.
- fact · [SkillsBench -- curated skills raise pass rates by 16.2pp; quality beats volume](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skillsbench-quality-evidence.md) — SkillsBench (Stanford/CMU/Berkeley/Oxford, arXiv 2602.12670) scored 47,150 public skills -- average quality 6.2/12, 73% elevated safety risk; curated skills +16.2pp pass rate, self-generated -1.3pp; focused 2-3 module skills +18.6pp vs larger bundles +5.9pp.
- person · [Aditi Saluja](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/person-aditi-saluja.md) — Authored the comprehensive Skills Registry and Catalog status/scope/roadmap document; mapping upstream/RHOAI skills landscape + NVIDIA secure supply chain.

- question · [Where do skill installation features live -- catalog, registry, or both?](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/question-skills-installation-features-location.md) — Open question needing its own STRAT -- skill installation could live in catalog (marketplace.json, npx), registry (APM, LOLA, mlflow CLI), or both. Different flows for catalog-sourced vs registry-sourced skills.
